g.text: · Calling list_remote_datasets
· Found 7 remote datasets.
A good dataset depends on what you want to measure and at what geography/time scale. Quick picks:
- City/county tracking (prices, rents, incomes)
- Zillow ZORI/ZHVI (rents/home values, monthly, ZIP–metro–state)
- Redfin Data Center (median sale price, inventory, new listings, metro/county)
- Census ACS (median household income, gross rent, owner costs; 1‑yr and 5‑yr; tract–state)
- Affordability burden metrics
- HUD CHAS (rent/owner cost burden shares, ≤30/50/80% AMI; tract–state)
- HUD Fair Market Rents + Income Limits (program benchmarks, county/metro)
- NLIHC Out of Reach (wage needed for 2BR FMR; state/county/metro)
- Indexes and controls
- FHFA House Price Index (repeat‑sales index; county/metro/state)
- Freddie Mac PMMS (mortgage rates)
- BLS CPI (Shelter/OER for real adjustments)
If you want microdata (household‑level rent burden, residual income): ACS PUMS via IPUMS.
Clarify:
1) Geography (tract, ZIP, county, metro, state, national)?
2) Metric (price‑to‑income, rent‑to‑income, residual income, burden thresholds, ownership vs rental)?
3) Period/frequency (last 5–10 years monthly/quarterly vs decennial view)?
I can spin up a “Housing Affordability Core” environment pulling ACS + HUD CHAS/FMR + Zillow/Redfin + FHFA + CPI/PMMS an
d document joins. Prefer that, or start with one source (e.g., ACS + ZORI) first?
